labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker gains access to principal with full service permissions
Enumerate all resources within the service
Create, modify, or delete any resource
Potentially pivot to other services via service-linked roles
labels.remediation: Replace wildcard actions with specific required actions. Scope resources to specific ARNs. Add conditions (e.g., aws:RequestedRegion, aws:ResourceTag).

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:PassRole and cloudformation:CreateStack permissions
Create a CloudFormation template that provisions IAM resources
Pass an admin role to CloudFormation as the service role
CloudFormation executes with admin privileges, creating backdoor resources
Attacker gains admin-equivalent access through the provisioned resources
labels.remediation: Restrict iam:PassRole to specific role ARNs. Limit cloudformation actions to specific stacks. Add aws:ResourceTag conditions.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has cloudformation:DeleteStack on wildcard
Delete critical infrastructure stacks
Cause service disruption and potential data loss
labels.remediation: Enable stack termination protection. Restrict DeleteStack to specific stack ARNs.

labels.label: secure
labels.remediation: N/A — this policy follows security best practices.

labels.remediation: Replace wildcard actions with specific required actions. Scope resources to specific ARNs. Add conditions (e.g., aws:RequestedRegion, aws:ResourceTag).

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has CloudFormation StackSets admin with PassRole and AssumeRole
Create a StackSet with malicious templates
Deploy across multiple accounts in the organization
Gain access to all target accounts simultaneously
labels.remediation: Restrict StackSet operations to specific admin roles. Limit target accounts and regions.

labels.label: secure
labels.remediation: N/A — this policy follows security best practices.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has cloudformation:UpdateStack on wildcard
Modify existing stack templates to inject malicious resources
Updated stack provisions backdoor access
labels.remediation: Restrict UpdateStack to specific stacks. Enable change sets for approval workflows.

labels.remediation: Replace wildcard actions with specific required actions. Scope resources to specific ARNs. Add conditions (e.g., aws:RequestedRegion, aws:ResourceTag).

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:AddUserToGroup on wildcard group resources
List IAM groups to find the admin group
Add their own user to the admin group
Inherit all admin group policies
labels.remediation: Restrict iam:AddUserToGroup to specific non-admin groups. Use SCPs to protect admin groups.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:AttachGroupPolicy on wildcard group resources
Attach AdministratorAccess to a group they belong to
Immediately gain full admin access through group membership
labels.remediation: Remove iam:AttachGroupPolicy. Use permission boundaries and SCPs.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:AttachRolePolicy on wildcard role resources
Attach AdministratorAccess to a role they can assume
Assume the role to gain full admin access
labels.remediation: Remove iam:AttachRolePolicy. Restrict to specific role ARNs if required.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:AttachUserPolicy on wildcard user resources
Attach the AdministratorAccess managed policy to their own user
Immediately gain full admin access to the account
labels.remediation: Remove iam:AttachUserPolicy. Use service control policies (SCPs) to prevent policy attachment. Implement permission boundaries.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:CreateAccessKey on wildcard user resources
List IAM users to find admin accounts
Create a new access key for an admin user
Use the access key for persistent admin access
labels.remediation: Restrict iam:CreateAccessKey to the user's own ARN (${aws:username}). Implement MFA conditions.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:CreateLoginProfile on wildcard user resources
Identify an admin user without console access
Create a login profile (console password) for that user
Log in to the console as the admin user
labels.remediation: Restrict iam:CreateLoginProfile to self. Add MFA requirements.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:CreatePolicyVersion on wildcard policy resources
Create a new version of an existing policy with admin permissions
Set the new version as the default
All principals using that policy now have admin access
labels.remediation: Remove iam:CreatePolicyVersion. Use AWS Organizations SCPs to prevent policy modification.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:PassRole and cloudformation:CreateStack permissions
Create a CloudFormation template that provisions IAM resources
Pass an admin role to CloudFormation as the service role
CloudFormation executes with admin privileges, creating backdoor resources
Attacker gains admin-equivalent access through the provisioned resources
labels.remediation: Restrict iam:PassRole to specific role ARNs. Limit cloudformation actions to specific stacks. Add aws:ResourceTag conditions.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:PassRole and codebuild:CreateProject permissions
Create a CodeBuild project with a malicious buildspec
Pass an admin service role to the project
Start a build — the build environment runs with admin privileges
Exfiltrate credentials from the build environment
labels.remediation: Restrict iam:PassRole to specific CodeBuild service role ARNs. Limit codebuild:CreateProject with conditions.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:PassRole and datapipeline:CreatePipeline permissions
Create a Data Pipeline with a malicious activity definition
Pass an admin role to the pipeline
Activate the pipeline — activities execute with admin privileges
labels.remediation: Restrict iam:PassRole to specific Data Pipeline role ARNs. Limit datapipeline:CreatePipeline.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:PassRole and ec2:RunInstances permissions
Create an instance profile with an admin role
Launch an EC2 instance with the admin instance profile
SSH into the instance and access the instance metadata service
Retrieve temporary admin credentials from IMDS
labels.remediation: Restrict iam:PassRole to specific instance profile role ARNs. Limit ec2:RunInstances by subnet, AMI, or instance type conditions.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:PassRole and glue:CreateDevEndpoint permissions
Create a Glue development endpoint
Pass an admin role to the endpoint
Connect to the endpoint and execute code with admin privileges
labels.remediation: Restrict iam:PassRole to specific Glue role ARNs. Limit glue:CreateDevEndpoint.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:PassRole and lambda:CreateFunction permissions
Create a Lambda function with malicious code
Pass an admin execution role to the function
Invoke the function — it runs with admin privileges
Exfiltrate credentials or modify IAM from within the Lambda
labels.remediation: Restrict iam:PassRole to specific Lambda execution role ARNs. Limit lambda:CreateFunction to specific function name patterns. Require resource tags.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:PassRole and lambda:UpdateFunctionCode permissions
Identify an existing Lambda function with a privileged execution role
Update the function code with malicious payload
Optionally pass a more privileged role via UpdateFunctionConfiguration
Invoke the function to execute code with elevated privileges
labels.remediation: Restrict lambda:UpdateFunctionCode to specific function ARNs. Restrict iam:PassRole to specific role ARNs. Separate update and invoke permissions.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:PassRole and sagemaker:CreateNotebookInstance permissions
Create a SageMaker notebook instance
Pass an admin execution role to the notebook
Open the notebook and execute code with admin privileges
labels.remediation: Restrict iam:PassRole to specific SageMaker role ARNs. Add VPC and tag conditions.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:PutGroupPolicy on wildcard group resources
Create an inline admin policy on a group they belong to
Gain full admin access through group membership
labels.remediation: Remove iam:PutGroupPolicy. Use managed policies only.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:PutRolePolicy on wildcard role resources
Create an inline admin policy on a role they can assume
Assume the role to gain full admin access
labels.remediation: Remove iam:PutRolePolicy. Restrict inline policy creation.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:PutUserPolicy on wildcard user resources
Create an inline policy granting admin access on their own user
Immediately gain full admin access
labels.remediation: Remove iam:PutUserPolicy. Use managed policies with strict version control.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:CreatePolicyVersion and iam:SetDefaultPolicyVersion
Create a new policy version with admin permissions
Set it as the default version
All principals using that policy are now admins
labels.remediation: Remove both iam:CreatePolicyVersion and iam:SetDefaultPolicyVersion. Implement change management for policies.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:UpdateAssumeRolePolicy on wildcard role resources
Modify the trust policy of an admin role
Add their own principal as a trusted entity
Assume the admin role
labels.remediation: Remove iam:UpdateAssumeRolePolicy. Protect trust policies with SCPs.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:UpdateLoginProfile on wildcard user resources
Reset the password for an admin user
Log in to the console as the admin user
labels.remediation: Restrict iam:UpdateLoginProfile to self (${aws:username}). Require MFA.

labels.label: secure
labels.remediation: N/A — this policy follows security best practices.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has scoped iam:AddUserToGroup permissions
Add users to groups to grant additional permissions
Potential for privilege escalation if admin groups are accessible
labels.remediation: Scope AddUserToGroup to specific non-privileged groups. Monitor group membership changes.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has scoped iam:PassRole permissions
Pass a role to an AWS service
Service operates with the passed role's permissions
labels.remediation: Restrict iam:PassRole to specific role ARNs and services via iam:PassedToService condition.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:PassRole and lambda create/update permissions
Create or modify a Lambda function
Pass a privileged execution role to the function
Invoke the function to run code with elevated privileges
labels.remediation: Restrict iam:PassRole to specific role ARNs. Limit Lambda operations to specific functions.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has sts:AssumeRole and sts:GetSessionToken on wildcard
Assume an initial role with limited privileges
From that role, assume another role with higher privileges
Chain multiple role assumptions to reach admin access
labels.remediation: Limit sts:AssumeRole to specific role ARNs. Implement session duration limits. Use aws:PrincipalTag conditions.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has cross-account sts:AssumeRole permissions
Assume a role in an external AWS account
Pivot laterally to access resources in the target account
Potentially chain further cross-account role assumptions
labels.remediation: Restrict to specific external account IDs. Add ExternalId conditions. Implement session policies.

labels.label: secure
labels.remediation: N/A — this policy follows security best practices.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has sts:AssumeRole on wildcard resources
Enumerate all roles in the account
Assume the most privileged role available
Gain elevated access through role assumption
labels.remediation: Restrict sts:AssumeRole to specific role ARNs. Add ExternalId or MFA conditions.

labels.label: secure
labels.remediation: N/A — this policy follows security best practices.

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has sts:GetFederationToken on wildcard
Generate federated credentials with a permissive session policy
Use federated credentials for access that bypasses normal controls
labels.remediation: Restrict sts:GetFederationToken to specific named sessions. Implement session policy restrictions.

labels.label: secure
labels.remediation: N/A — this policy follows security best practices.

labels.label: secure

labels.label: vulnerable
attack_path: Attacker has iam:* on all resources
Create new admin users, roles, and policies at will
Modify any existing IAM configuration
Complete control over the account's identity and access management
labels.remediation: Never grant iam:* to non-admin users. Use least-privilege IAM permissions. Implement permission boundaries and SCPs.
